data.title: Comcast Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 beats by $0.06, revenue of $31.46B beats by $1.11B
data.description:
• Comcast press release (CMCSA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.79 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $31.46B (+5.3% Y/Y) beats by $1.11B.
  • Generated Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA of $7.9 Billion, Adjusted EPS of $0.79 and Free Cash Flow of $3.9 Billion.
  • Net Cash Provided by Operating Activities was $6.9 billion. Free Cash Flow was $3.9 billion.
  • Peacock Delivered Strong Growth with Paid Subscribers Increasing 12% Year-over-Year to 46 Million and Revenue Growth of 71%, Surpassing $2 Billion for the First Time
  • Theme Parks EBITDA Increased 33% to $551 Million, Fueled by the Opening of Epic Universe in May 2025

data.title: UK telecom firms warn of mobile signal rationing amid energy crisis - report
data.description:
UK telecom operators have warned that they may be forced to ration mobile signal access as the Iran war drives energy prices higher, with some companies drawing up contingency plans to reduce costs, The Telegraph reported.

Companies such as VodafoneThree, Virgin Media O2 and BT-owned EE issued the warning to the government after being excluded from a major energy support scheme.

The contingency plans include options like rationing access to mobile networks, slowing down speeds to reduce energy use, and surge pricing.

The companies also warned that they may be forced to scale back plans to expand their 5G networks, which could lead to jobs either being cut or moved offshore.

The UK government unveiled the British Industrial Competitiveness Scheme last week, under which electricity bills for over 10,000 energy-intensive manufacturers would be cut by up to 25% next year.

But telecom firms were excluded from the support scheme. These companies believe their sector must be included as they operate critical national infrastructure.

Mobile networks consume just under 1 terawatt-hour of electricity annually, equivalent to powering 370,000 homes a year.

According to telecom executives, the sector is disproportionately exposed to the war-driven energy crisis due to the "always on" nature of mobile networks – meaning they can't shift demand to cheaper, off-peak times.

VodafoneThree said it was disappointed by the exclusion from the scheme. "We urge the government to consider the impact of rising energy prices on the vital telecoms sector that unlocks growth in all parts of the economy," a spokesperson said.

BT and Virgin Media O2 said they had no plans to ration access to their networks, but warned that rising energy prices could hinder long-term investment in the UK.

Mobile network operators are required by law to maintain connectivity, a UK government spokesperson told The Telegraph.

If telecom operators decided to ration access to mobile signals, it would worsen internet speed in the UK, which already ranks last among G7 nations for 5G download speeds.

data.title: Dover Non-GAAP EPS of $2.28 beats by $0.01, revenue of $2.05B beats by $50M
data.description:
• Dover press release (DOV): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.28 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $2.05B (+9.6% Y/Y) beats by $50M.
  • In 2026, Dover expects to generate GAAP EPS in the range of $8.92 to $9.12 (adjusted EPS of $10.45 to $10.65), (vs. consensus of $10.58) based on full year revenue growth of 5% to 7% (organic growth of 3% to 5%).

data.title: Ryder Non-GAAP EPS of $2.54 beats by $0.27, revenue of $3.13B misses by $10M
data.description:
• Ryder press release (R): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.54 beats by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $3.13B (flat Y/Y) misses by $10M.

  • Full Year 2026 Forecast

    • ROE (non-GAAP) unchanged at 17% - 18% 
    • Comparable EPS (non-GAAP) increased to $14.05 - $14.80 vs. $14.01 consensus
    • Operating revenue (non-GAAP) growth remains at 3%, primarily driven by SCS
    • Net cash provided by operating activities from continuing operations remains at $2.7 billion and free cash flow (non-GAAP) unchanged at $700 million - $800 million

data.title: Cemex GAAP EPS of $0.17 beats by $0.06, revenue of $4.02B beats by $160M; gives FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Cemex press release (CX): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.17 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $4.02B (+11.4% Y/Y) beats by $160M.

data.title: Thermo Fisher outlook in focus as stock drops despite Q1 beat
data.description:
Thermo Fisher (TMO) shares fell ~4% in the premarket on Thursday, despite posting better-than-expected Q1 2026 results, as investors await the life sciences company's full-year outlook, expected to be provided during its earnings call, scheduled for 8:30 a.m. ET.

The Waltham, Massachusetts-based healthcare giant did exceed Street forecasts with its Q1 results, as its revenue jumped ~6% YoY to $11.0B, mainly due to its Life Sciences Solutions segment, beating the consensus by $150M.

However, the company’s non-GAAP organic revenue growth reached 1% YoY, missing the analyst estimates of 1.2% YoY, according to Bloomberg data.

Revenue from its Life Sciences Solutions unit rose ~13% YoY to $2.6B, while Thermo’s (TMO) Analytical Instruments and Specialty Diagnostics units contracted, adding $1.7B and $1.1B to the topline, respectively. However, its Laboratory Products and Biopharma Services segment expanded by ~7%, with $6.0B in revenue.

Meanwhile, TMO’s adjusted earnings per share spiked ~6% YoY to $5.44, topping the estimates by $0.20. Its adjusted operating income rose ~6% YoY to $2.4B, and the adjusted operating margin reached 21.8%, with a 10 bps decline driven by the analytical instruments segment, where the segment margin reached 20.7% from 23.7% in the prior-year period.

data.title: MarineMax Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.01, revenue of $527.4M misses by $88.35M
data.description:
• MarineMax press release (HZO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.04 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $527.4M (-16.5% Y/Y) misses by $88.35M.
  • This decline, primarily driven by lower boat sales, was partially offset by continued growth in higher-margin businesses, including finance and insurance, superyacht services and marinas.
  • Same-store sales decreased 15% due to challenging environment, compared to an increase of 11% in the prior-year period
  • Based on current business conditions, retail marine industry trends, and other relevant factors, the Company continues to expect fiscal 2026 Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $110 million to $125 million and adjusted net income in the range of $0.40 to $0.95 per diluted share vs. $0.74 consensus

data.title: FirstCash Non-GAAP EPS of $2.69 beats by $0.30, revenue of $1.05B beats by $40M
data.description:
• FirstCash press release (FCFS): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.69 beats by $0.30.
  • Revenue of $1.05B (+25.5% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • The company also announced that the Board of Directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.42 per share, which will be paid in May 2026.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the first quarter increased 29% to $211 million compared to the prior-year quarter.

  • 2026 Outlook

    The outlook for the remainder of 2026 continues to be highly positive. The company is raising its expectations for year-over-year growth in pawn segment revenues, driven by the continued growth in same-store pawn receivables and better than anticipated revenue contributions from stores acquired in 2025.

data.title: IBM slides on unchanged outlook, ranks as most overvalued among IT consulting firms
data.description:
IBM (IBM) shares tumbled 7% despite beating first-quarter estimates on nearly every metric, a sharp market verdict on management's decision to leave its full-year guidance unchanged even after posting adjusted EPS of $1.91, revenue of $15.92 billion, and free cash flow of $2.22 billion, all comfortably above Wall Street's expectations.

Despite being the largest company on the list, IBM ranks at the bottom of this valuation ranking among IT consulting and other services stocks above $2B market cap. It carries only a C+ valuation grade, trailing peers such as DXC Technology (DXC) and Kyndryl (KD), which both earn A+ grades, as well as Amdocs (DOX) and EPAM Systems (EPAM), which hold A- grades. 

In other words, investors may still view IBM as fundamentally solid, but not especially cheap relative to its growth outlook and sector peers.

Against that backdrop, the following list ranks IT consulting and other services stocks by valuation grade, which measures how expensive or inexpensive a stock appears relative to its fundamentals. An A+ grade signals the most undervalued names, while lower grades indicate stocks that trade at richer multiples.

Here is the list:

  1. DXC Technology Company (DXC), Valuation grade: A+

  2. Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (KD), Valuation grade: A+

  3. Amdocs Limited (DOX), Valuation grade: A-

  4. EPAM Systems, Inc. (EPAM), Valuation grade: A-

  5. Cognizant Technology Solutions Corporation (CTSH), Valuation grade: B+

  6. Gartner, Inc. (IT), Valuation grade: B+

  7. International Business Machines Corporation (IBM), Valuation grade: C+

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Mobileye to buy back $250M of shares
data.description:
Mobileye (MBLY) on Thursday said its board authorized a share repurchase program of up to $250M of the company’s outstanding Class A common stock.

The buyback is intended to partially offset dilution due to share-based compensation and shares issued in connection with the Mentee Robotics acquisition, while maintaining flexibility to invest in its core automotive business and Physical AI platform, Mobileye said in a statement. 

Repurchases may be made from time to time through open-market purchases, privately negotiated deals, or accelerated share repurchase agreements, subject to market conditions and other factors.

“Our primary capital allocation priority remains investing in R&D and capital expenditures to support innovation and execute on our expanding product roadmap," said Mobileye CEO.

Mobileye said it does not intend to directly repurchase shares of its Class A or Class B common stock for cash from Intel or other related parties, under the buyback program. 

Intel (INTC) owns a stake in the Israeli company that develops autonomous driving technology and advanced driver assistance systems. 

Mobileye said it expects to fund the repurchase program through existing cash on hand and future cash flows.Shares up +2.91% in premarket trading. The company is also reporting its Q1 earnings on Thursday.
